title: andropogon chordatus
intro: Andropogon chordatus, also known as East Indian grass or East Indian sweet grass, is a type of grass native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of East Indian grass include reducing stress, anxiety, and promoting relaxation, as well as treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to relieve stress, improve mood, and promote overall well-being.
